title: rubus gressittii
intro: Rubus gressittii, also known as the Taiwan raspberry or Formosan raspberry, is a species of raspberry native to Taiwan and southern China. It is a type of bramble, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herbal preparations of Rubus gressittii typically involve drying the fruits or leaves to make tea, decoctions, or infusions that can be consumed to reap its health benefits.
